body:
HIGH, erasure-integrity. Found by the T62 prosecutor (2026-07-22 overnight). Affects LANDED code (eraseImpl, from T55) as well as T62's eraseReplicaImpl, so main currently has this leak under a mirror.

THE BUG. `assertBytesGone` (src/gateway/erase.ts) decides §11 completeness with `gw.backend.deltasSince(new Set())` gated on `removed === 0`. Under `MirrorBackend` this is TIER-BLIND:

1. `MirrorBackend.deltasSince` (mirror.ts:69-71) returns ONLY the primary tier. A byte still at rest on the MIRROR tier is never in the set the verdict inspects — erase reports complete while the mirror keeps the byte.

2. `MirrorBackend.purge` (mirror.ts:87-97) returns `Math.max(primary, mirror)`. If the primary silently retains (returns 0, keeps the row) while the mirror legitimately removes a straggler (returns 1), `removed = max(0,1) = 1`, so the `removed === 0` guard SKIPS the scan entirely — even though a scan of the primary would have seen the retained byte.

Both are H7 (report success without proving the bytes went), both violate §11's "every tier" promise. Next-boot `heal(exclude)` eventually sweeps the mirror, but the synchronous erase has already returned a false completion to the data subject / compliance record.

THE FIX. The verdict must inspect the SAME set of tiers §11 requires the bytes gone from, independent of the aggregate purge count. Two shapes:
  (i) Add an all-tiers byte-presence probe to StoreBackend — e.g. `holds(id: string): Promise<boolean>`, implemented per driver with a TARGETED lookup (sqlite: SELECT WHERE id; memory: map.has; archive: file-exists across fans incl. .tmp stragglers; local-storage: key + quarantine scan; mirror: `primary.holds(id) || mirror.holds(id)`; quarantine-pool backend as appropriate). Then `assertBytesGone` becomes `if (await gw.backend.holds(id)) throw`, dropping the `removed === 0` gate entirely (erasures are rare; a targeted probe is cheap).
  (ii) Or expose per-tier retention from MirrorBackend so assertBytesGone asserts each tier clean.

(i) is preferred — it makes byte-presence a first-class backend question and reuses the archive's straggler-aware sweep semantics (the .tmp stragglers T40/T55 taught purge to see; `holds` must see them too, or a stranded .tmp reports absent while the bytes remain — mirror the archive purge's prefix logic, do NOT use deltasSince which skips .tmp by design).

RAILS — BOTH LEVELS, and the object level is the FILESYSTEM/tier. Build a MirrorBackend(primary, mirror) where the MIRROR retains on purge (returns 0, keeps the row) and the primary is clean: erase must REFUSE (not report complete). And the inverse: primary retains, mirror removes a straggler (returns 1): erase must still REFUSE despite the positive aggregate count. Plus the archive-straggler case: a `.tmp` straggler on a tier must make `holds` true so erase refuses. Probe each: reverting the tier-complete probe to primary-only deltasSince must turn each red.

CARE: erasure-critical, and it touches the StoreBackend CONTRACT (6 drivers). Prosecute hard. A wrong `holds` reports the exact false completion it exists to prevent, so the rails must be byte/tier level, never API-level. This is a REPAIR of stated §11 behavior (no on-wire change), but given the blast radius consider Myk's eye on the `holds` semantics before merge. It should not sit long — it is a live erasure leak on main under a mirror.
title: Erasure completeness is TIER-BLIND under MirrorBackend — the byte-presence verdict reads only the primary (live leak on main)
